seoTitle: How to Set Up the Chart of Accounts in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Build your Chart of Accounts in QuickBooks Online from scratch, set account types, create sub-accounts, and import existing ledgers via CSV.
intro: Your Chart of Accounts is the backbone of your entire bookkeeping setup, so getting it structured correctly from the start matters. This guide walks through creating accounts, organizing them into sub-accounts, and importing an existing ledger if you're migrating data.

steps.heading: Open the Chart of Accounts
steps.body: Go to the Accounting section in the left-hand sidebar and select Chart of Accounts to view your existing ledger structure.

steps.heading: Create a new account
steps.body: Click the green New button, then choose the appropriate account type (such as asset, liability, income, or expense) and a matching detail type.

steps.heading: Name and classify the account
steps.body: Enter a clear category name in the Name field so the account is easy to identify later.

steps.heading: Set up sub-accounts if needed
steps.body: Check the 'Is sub-account' box and select a parent category to nest a new line item under a broader account.

steps.heading: Add opening balances
steps.body: Enter historical opening balances and the corresponding as-of date if you're setting up an account with existing history.

steps.heading: Import accounts in bulk via CSV
steps.body: Click the dropdown arrow next to New and select Import to upload a spreadsheet of accounts, then map your columns to QuickBooks fields to complete the import.

faq.question: Can I import my whole Chart of Accounts instead of typing each one in?
faq.answer: Yes, you can drag and drop a CSV spreadsheet into the import tool and map your columns to QuickBooks fields to create many accounts at once.

faq.question: What's the difference between an account type and a detail type?
faq.answer: Account type is the broad category (like asset or expense), while detail type is a more specific classification within that category.

faq.question: Should I use account numbers?
faq.answer: Enabling account numbers in Advanced Company Settings can help keep a larger Chart of Accounts sorted and easier to navigate.

faq.question: Can I nest accounts under other accounts?
faq.answer: Yes, checking the 'Is sub-account' box lets you link a new account to a parent category to build a hierarchy.
